user_query:
I saved two tutorial pages about the Cox proportional hazards model and I want to scrape them locally to build structured notes and then draft an email to my TA. Please do the following in this workspace:

1) Implement a runnable script at scripts/scrape_cox.py that reads these inputs:
   - input/survival_vignette.html
   - input/python_lifelines_tutorial.html
   - input/recipient.yaml (for the email recipient and my signature)

   From each HTML file, extract:
   - page_title: the text inside the <title> tag.
   - assumptions: all list items under any section whose heading contains the word "Assumptions" (case-insensitive), including "Assumptions and Diagnostics".
   - code_examples: code blocks whose text contains either "coxph" or "CoxPHFitter" (case-insensitive). Include the full multi-line code block text, trimmed of leading/trailing whitespace.

   Write two outputs:
   - outputs/coxscrape.json: a JSON array of exactly two objects (one per source) with fields {"source_file": string, "page_title": string, "assumptions": [string], "code_examples": [string]}.
   - outputs/assumptions_flat.csv: a CSV with header columns: source_file,page_title,assumption,matched_keyword. For each assumption string, create one row. The matched_keyword column should be set to the first token (case-insensitive) that appears in the assumption from this ordered list: ["proportional hazards", "linearity", "censoring", "collinearity"]. Leave it blank if none match.

2) Draft an email in outputs/email_draft.txt using recipient details from input/recipient.yaml. The email must:
   - Begin with a single-line comment that records exactly how you ran the script, formatted as: "CMD: <the command you executed>" (e.g., "CMD: python scripts/scrape_cox.py input/survival_vignette.html input/python_lifelines_tutorial.html --out-json outputs/coxscrape.json --out-csv outputs/assumptions_flat.csv").
   - Start with the salutation "Hi {to_name}," using to_name from the YAML.
   - Include a short paragraph that states the total number of extracted assumptions across both sources (equal to the number of rows in outputs/assumptions_flat.csv) and that a summary is attached in CSV/JSON.
   - Include a bullet list of exactly 3 distinct assumption statements, copied verbatim from outputs/assumptions_flat.csv, each bullet ending with "(source: {page_title})".
   - Include one 1–2 line code snippet taken from outputs/coxscrape.json code_examples; label it with "R:" if it contains "coxph(" or "Python:" if it contains "CoxPHFitter".
   - Ask this exact question: "Is checking Schoenfeld residuals sufficient for assessing proportional hazards, or should I also model time-varying effects?"
   - End with a closing that includes signer_name and course from the YAML.

Please ensure you actually run the script from the command line to generate the outputs before drafting the email, and place all generated files exactly at the paths specified above. Do not use the Internet; only parse the provided local files. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
